---
title: "Lecture 12: JavaScript Variables & Data Types"
subtitle: "{{< meta course.number >}} - {{< meta course.title >}}"
author: "{{< meta instructor.name >}}"
date: "February 12, 2026"
code-annotations: below
format: 
  revealjs:
    slide-number: true
    code-link: true
    highlight-style: a11y
    chalkboard: true
    transition: slide
    transition-speed: default
    code-line-numbers: true
    code-overflow: wrap
    theme: 
      - ../course-slides-styles.scss
---

## {#title-slide data-menu-title="Title Slide" background="#053660"}

<div class="custom-title">
[Lecture 12]{.baby-blue-text}
</div>

<div class="custom-subtitle">
[JavaScript Variables & Data Types]{.white-text}
</div>

<div class="custom-subtitle3">
Week 3 | Unit II: CSS Styling & JavaScript<br>
{{< meta course.number >}} - {{< meta course.title >}}<br>
{{< meta instructor.name >}}, {{< meta instructor.title >}}
</div>

---

## {data-menu-title="Learning Objectives"}

[Learning Objectives]{.slide-title}

<hr>

::: {.incremental .body-text-m}
- Understand JavaScript and its role in web development
- Declare and use variables properly
- Work with primitive and complex data types
- Use operators in expressions
- Understand variable scope
- Practice JavaScript fundamentals
:::

::: {.notes}
JavaScript is what makes web pages interactive. Variables and data types are the foundation of any programming language.
:::

---

## {data-menu-title="What is JavaScript?"}}

[JavaScript: The Language of the Web]{.slide-title}

<hr>

::: {.body-text-m}
::: {.fragment}
- Programming language that runs in browsers
:::
::: {.fragment}
- Adds **interactivity** to web pages
:::
::: {.fragment}
- Currently the **only language** that runs natively in browsers
:::
::: {.fragment}
- Can also run on servers (Node.js)
:::
:::

::: {.fragment}
**Remember:**
```
HTML = Structure
CSS  = Styling
JS   = Behavior & Interactivity
```
:::

---

## {data-menu-title="JavaScript in HTML"}}

[Including JavaScript in HTML]{.slide-title}

<hr>

**Inline (not recommended):**
```html
<button onclick="alert('Clicked!')">Click Me</button>
```

**Internal script tag:**
```html
<script>
    console.log("Hello from JavaScript!");
</script>
```

**External file (best practice):**
```html
<script src="script.js"></script>
```

::: {.fragment .body-text-m .center-text .topbr}
[Always put `<script>` at end of `<body>` tag]{.teal-text}
:::

---

## {data-menu-title="Variables"}}

[Declaring Variables]{.slide-title}

<hr>

Variables store data. Declare with `let`, `const`, or `var`:

```{.javascript code-line-numbers="|1|2|3"}
let name = "Alice";       /* Can be reassigned */
const age = 25;           /* Cannot be reassigned */
var city = "New York";    /* Older way (avoid) */
```

::: {.body-text-m .topbr}
**Best practices:**
- Use `const` by default
- Use `let` if value changes
- Avoid `var` (old syntax)
:::

::: {.fragment}
```javascript
const pi = 3.14159;      /* Never changes */
let count = 0;           /* Will increase */
```
:::

---

## {data-menu-title="Naming Rules"}}

[Variable Naming Rules]{.slide-title}

<hr>

::: {.incremental .body-text-m}
- Must start with letter, `_`, or `$`
- Can contain letters, numbers, `_`, `$`
- Case-sensitive (`name` vs `Name`)
- Use camelCase: `firstName`, `myVariable`
- Avoid reserved words: `let`, `const`, `if`, etc.
- Choose descriptive names
:::

**Good examples:**
```javascript
const firstName = "John";
let userCount = 5;
const MAX_USERS = 100;
```

**Bad examples:**
```javascript
const x = 5;              /* Not descriptive */
const my-name = "John";   /* Hyphen not allowed */
const 123abc = 10;        /* Can't start with number */
```

---

## {data-menu-title="Primitive Types"}}

[Primitive Data Types]{.slide-title}

<hr>

Basic types that hold single values:

::: {.body-text-m}
::: {.fragment}
[**String**]{.teal-text} - Text
```javascript
const name = "Alice";
const message = 'Hello world';
```
:::
::: {.fragment}
[**Number**]{.teal-text} - Integers and decimals
```javascript
const age = 25;
const price = 19.99;
```
:::
::: {.fragment}
[**Boolean**]{.teal-text} - true or false
```javascript
const isStudent = true;
const isLoggedIn = false;
```
:::
:::

---

## {data-menu-title="More Primitives"}}

[More Primitive Types]{.slide-title}

<hr>

::: {.body-text-m}
::: {.fragment}
[**undefined**]{.teal-text} - Variable declared but not assigned
```javascript
let x;  /* undefined */
```
:::
::: {.fragment}
[**null**]{.teal-text} - Intentional emptiness
```javascript
let data = null;
```
:::
::: {.fragment}
[**Symbol**]{.teal-text} - Unique identifier (advanced)
```javascript
const id = Symbol('unique');
```
:::
:::

---

## {data-menu-title="Arrays"}}

[Arrays (Complex Type)]{.slide-title}

<hr>

Ordered collection of values:

```{.javascript code-line-numbers="|1|2|3|4"}
const fruits = ["apple", "banana", "orange"];
const numbers = [1, 2, 3, 4, 5];
const mixed = [1, "hello", true, null];

console.log(fruits[0]);  /* "apple" */
console.log(fruits.length); /* 3 */
```

**Array methods:**
```javascript
fruits.push("grape");         /* Add to end */
fruits.pop();                 /* Remove last */
let first = fruits[0];        /* Access by index */
```

---

## {data-menu-title="Objects"}}

[Objects (Complex Type)]{.slide-title}

<hr>

Key-value pairs:

```{.javascript code-line-numbers="|1-6|7|8|9"}
const person = {
    name: "Alice",
    age: 25,
    city: "New York",
    isStudent: true
};

console.log(person.name);      /* "Alice" */
console.log(person["age"]);    /* 25 */
person.age = 26;               /* Modify property */
```

::: {.fragment .body-text-m .center-text .topbr}
[Objects are key in JavaScript programming!]{.magenta-text}
:::

---

## {data-menu-title="Operators"}}

[JavaScript Operators]{.slide-title}

<hr>

::: columns
::: {.column width="50%"}
[**Arithmetic**]{.teal-text}

```js
5 + 3    // 8
5 - 3    // 2
5 * 3    // 15
5 / 3    // 1.666...
5 % 3    // 2 (remainder)
2 ** 3   // 8 (power)
```
:::

::: {.column width="50%"}
[**Comparison**]{.teal-text}

```js
5 > 3    // true
5 < 3    // false
5 >= 5   // true
5 == 5   // true
5 === 5  // true (strict)
5 != 3   // true
```
:::
:::

---

## {data-menu-title="Logical Operators"}}

[Logical Operators]{.slide-title}

<hr>

Combine boolean values:

```{.javascript code-line-numbers="|1|2|3"}
true && false   /* AND - both must be true */
true || false   /* OR - at least one true */
!true           /* NOT - reverses boolean */
```

**Practical example:**
```javascript
const age = 25;
const hasLicense = true;

if (age >= 18 && hasLicense) {
    console.log("Can drive");
}
```

---

## {data-menu-title="Type Conversion"}}

[Type Conversion & Coercion]{.slide-title}

<hr>

Converting between types:

```{.javascript code-line-numbers="|1|2|3|4|5"}
String(25)           /* "25" - to string */
Number("25")         /* 25 - to number */
Boolean(1)           /* true - to boolean */
parseInt("25")       /* 25 - string to integer */
parseFloat("25.99")  /* 25.99 - string to float */
```

**Automatic coercion (watch out!):**
```javascript
"5" + 3      /* "53" (concatenation) */
"5" - 3      /* 2 (subtraction) */
"5" == 5     /* true (loose equality) */
"5" === 5    /* false (strict equality) */
```

---

## {data-menu-title="String Operations"}}

[Working with Strings]{.slide-title}

<hr>

```{.javascript code-line-numbers="|1|2|3|4|5"}
const text = "Hello World";

text.length           /* 11 */
text.toUpperCase()    /* "HELLO WORLD" */
text.toLowerCase()    /* "hello world" */
text.substring(0, 5)  /* "Hello" */
text.includes("World") /* true */
text.replace("World", "JS") /* "Hello JS" */
```

**String concatenation:**
```javascript
const first = "John";
const last = "Doe";
const full = first + " " + last;  /* "John Doe" */

/* Template literals (easier) */
const full2 = `${first} ${last}`;
```

---

## {data-menu-title="Variable Scope"}}

[Variable Scope]{.slide-title}

<hr>

Where variables are accessible:

```{.javascript code-line-numbers="|1|2-5|6|7"}
const global = "accessible everywhere";

function myFunction() {
    const local = "only inside function";
    console.log(global);  /* Works */
}
console.log(local);       /* Error - not defined outside */
```

**Block scope:**
```javascript
if (true) {
    let x = 5;           /* Only inside block */
}
console.log(x);          /* Error - not accessible */
```

---

## {data-menu-title="Console Output"}}

[Console Methods for Debugging]{.slide-title}

<hr>

```{.javascript code-line-numbers="|1|2|3|4|5"}
console.log("Regular message");      /* Basic output */
console.error("Error message");      /* Red error text */
console.warn("Warning message");     /* Yellow warning */
console.table([1, 2, 3]);           /* Table format */
console.clear();                     /* Clear console */
```

**Debugging tip:**
```javascript
const person = { name: "Alice", age: 25 };
console.log(person);
```

---

## {data-menu-title="Practical Example"}}

[Practical JavaScript Example]{.slide-title}

<hr>

```{.javascript code-line-numbers="|1-4|6-10|12-14"}
// Declare variables
const student = {
    name: "Alice",
    grades: [85, 90, 78, 92]
};

// Calculate average
let sum = student.grades[0] + student.grades[1] + 
          student.grades[2] + student.grades[3];
let average = sum / student.grades.length;

// Output result
console.log(`${student.name}'s average: ${average.toFixed(1)}`);
/* Output: Alice's average: 86.3 */
```

---

## {data-menu-title="Resources"}}

[Resources & References]{.slide-title}

<hr>

::: {.body-text-m}
- [MDN: JavaScript Guide](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/JavaScript/Guide)
- [MDN: Variables](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Learn/JavaScript/First_steps/Variables)
- [MDN: Data Types](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/JavaScript/Data_structures)
- [Course content: Week 3](/content/week-03.qmd)
:::

---

## {#end-slide data-menu-title="End Slide" background="#053660"}

<div class="page-center">
<div class="custom-subtitle">
[Questions?]{.white-text}
</div>
<div class="custom-subtitle3">
Next: Week 4 - JavaScript Functions & Events
</div>
</div>
